lemma_statement.name: restricted_continuation_boundary_flux_pairing_discharge_lemma
lemma_statement.claim: On the restricted-continuation boundary portion partial Sigma_R, the certified trace pairing identifies with the boundary flux pairing whenever the explicitly listed flux-identification, orientation, and sign-convention compatibility hypotheses are supplied.

required_inputs.name: restricted_continuation_trace_pairing_exists
required_inputs.role: supplies the already certified trace pairing over partial Sigma_R

required_inputs.name: flux_field_matches_normal_trace_payload
required_inputs.role: identifies the normal trace object with the restricted-continuation flux payload

required_inputs.name: boundary_orientation_flux_convention_compatible
required_inputs.role: ensures the trace pairing and flux pairing use the same outward-normal orientation convention

required_inputs.name: boundary_flux_pairing_operator_available
required_inputs.role: supplies the boundary flux pairing map on the certified trace/normal-trace spaces

required_inputs.name: restricted_boundary_measure_support_compatible
required_inputs.role: ensures the pairing is supported exactly on the restricted boundary portion partial Sigma_R

discharged_obligations: Separates boundary flux pairing from scalar boundary trace existence.
Separates boundary flux pairing from normal trace existence.
Separates boundary flux pairing from bare trace pairing.
Certifies the exact assumptions under which the restricted-continuation boundary flux pairing is well-defined.
Promotes boundary flux pairing from an undischarged target obligation to a conditional discharge lemma.
non_claims: Does not prove flux sign control.
Does not prove restricted integration by parts.
Does not prove the energy inequality.
Does not prove full RR closure.
